guide.title: NSF 53 vs 58 is not enough by itself for PFAS decisions
guide.lede: The standard code matters, but the project should still check the exact claim, model, replacement cadence, and cost before treating a product as a fit.
guide.nextActionTitle: Read the claim before reading the badge
guide.nextActionSummary: A product should only open if the exact model has direct PFAS claim support and the maintenance burden still makes sense for the household.
whyThis: Standard numbers without claim scope can be misleading.
Claim-level support matters more than broad category language.
Replacement burden can matter as much as the upfront badge.
whatThisDoesNotTellYou: This guide does not make one standard universally better for everyone.
It does not replace model-specific listing verification.
It does not mean certification equals every current regulatory benchmark.
checklist: Check replacement cadence and annual ownership cost.
